set.seed(8675309)
options(scipen = 999, timeout = 600)

theme_set(theme_minimal())
theme_update(
  plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
  panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
  strip.background = element_rect(fill = "black"),

  panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),

)

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (Download and load raw data files)"}
zenodo_url <- "https://zenodo.org/records/20119134/files/digital-wellbeing/open-play-v1.2.1.zip?download=1"
zip_path <- "data/open-play-v1.2.1.zip"

if (!file.exists(zip_path)) {
  dir.create(dirname(zip_path), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  options(timeout = max(600, getOption("timeout")))

  message("Downloading 194MB file from Zenodo (may take a few minutes)...")
  tryCatch(
    {
      download.file(zenodo_url, zip_path, mode = "wb", method = "libcurl")
      message("Download complete!")
    },
    error = function(e) {
      message("Download failed. Please download manually from:")
      message(zenodo_url)
      message(glue("And save to: {normalizePath(zip_path, mustWork = FALSE)}"))
      stop(e)
    }
  )
}

# Extract zip if not already extracted
extract_dir <- "data/open-play-v1.2.1"
if (!dir.exists(extract_dir)) {
  message("Extracting zip archive...")
  unzip(zip_path, exdir = "data")
  # Rename the extracted folder to a simpler name
  extracted_folder <- list.dirs("data", recursive = FALSE, full.names = TRUE)
  extracted_folder <- extracted_folder[grepl(
    "digital-wellbeing-open-play",
    extracted_folder
  )]
  if (length(extracted_folder) == 1 && extracted_folder != extract_dir) {
    file.rename(extracted_folder, extract_dir)
  }
}

# Read raw data files
intake <- read_csv(
  file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/survey_intake.csv.gz"),
  guess_max = 10000
)

biweekly <- readr::read_csv(
  file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/survey_biweekly.csv.gz"),
  guess_max = 10000
)

surveys <- read_csv(
  file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/survey_daily.csv.gz")
) |>
  filter(pid %in% intake$pid)


biweekly <- read_csv(
  file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/survey_biweekly.csv.gz"),
  guess_max = 10000
)

xbox <- read_csv(file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/xbox.csv.gz"))
nintendo <- read_csv(file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/nintendo.csv.gz"))
steam <- read_csv(
  file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/steam.csv.gz"),
  guess_max = 10000
)
games <- read_csv(
  file.path(extract_dir, "data/clean/game_metadata.csv.gz"),
  guess_max = 10000
)

# Load helper functions from the dataset (includes get_dst_offset for timezone handling)
source(file.path(extract_dir, "R/helpers.R"))
```
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (Process telemetry into session, hourly, daily, and weekly aggregations)"}
# Cache directory for processed telemetry (speeds up re-renders)
cache_dir <- "data/cache"
cache_file <- file.path(cache_dir, "telemetry_cache.rds")

if (file.exists(cache_file)) {
  # Load cached objects
  message("Loading cached telemetry data...")
  cache <- readRDS(cache_file)
  sessions_telemetry <- cache$sessions_telemetry
  hourly_telemetry <- cache$hourly_telemetry
  daily_telemetry <- cache$daily_telemetry
  weekly_telemetry <- cache$weekly_telemetry
  telemetry_spans <- cache$telemetry_spans
  rm(cache)
} else {
  message("Processing telemetry data (this may take a few minutes)...")

  # Map participants to their local timezone (for DST-aware conversion)
  tz_map <- intake |>
    mutate(
      pid = as.character(pid),
      country,
      local_timezone,
      .keep = "none"
    ) |>
    distinct(pid, .keep_all = TRUE)

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # SESSION-LEVEL (Nintendo + Xbox only; Steam doesn't provide session data)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  sessions_telemetry <- bind_rows(
    xbox |> mutate(platform = "Xbox"),
    nintendo |> mutate(platform = "Nintendo")
  ) |>
    left_join(tz_map, by = "pid") |>
    filter(!is.na(local_timezone)) |>
    mutate(
      offset_start = get_dst_offset(session_start, country, local_timezone),
      offset_end = get_dst_offset(session_end, country, local_timezone),
      start_local = session_start + offset_start,
      end_local = session_end + offset_end,
      duration_min = as.numeric(difftime(
        session_end,
        session_start,
        units = "mins"
      ))
    ) |>
    filter(
      !is.na(session_start),
      !is.na(session_end),
      session_end > session_start,
      duration_min >= 1,
      title_id != "Wallpaper Engine" # not a game, but a desktop customization tool
    )

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # HOURLY (all platforms)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  hourly_from_sessions <- sessions_telemetry |>
    filter(!is.na(start_local), !is.na(end_local)) |>
    mutate(
      h0_local = floor_date(start_local, "hour"),
      h1_local = floor_date(end_local - seconds(1), "hour"),
      n_hours = as.integer(difftime(h1_local, h0_local, units = "hours")) + 1
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(n_hours), n_hours > 0) |>
    tidyr::uncount(n_hours, .remove = FALSE, .id = "k") |>
    mutate(
      hour_start_local = h0_local + hours(k - 1),
      minutes = pmax(
        0,
        as.numeric(difftime(
          pmin(end_local, hour_start_local + hours(1)),
          pmax(start_local, hour_start_local),
          units = "mins"
        ))
      ),
      hour_start_utc = with_tz(hour_start_local, tzone = "UTC")
    ) |>
    select(
      pid,
      platform,
      title_id,
      hour_start_local,
      hour_start_utc,
      minutes
    ) |>
    group_by(pid, platform, title_id, hour_start_local, hour_start_utc) |>
    summarise(minutes = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  hourly_from_steam <- steam |>
    select(pid, title_id, datetime_hour_start, minutes) |>
    mutate(pid = as.character(pid)) |>
    left_join(tz_map, by = "pid") |>
    filter(!is.na(local_timezone)) |>
    mutate(
      platform = "Steam",
      hour_start_utc = datetime_hour_start,
      offset = get_dst_offset(datetime_hour_start, country, local_timezone),
      hour_start_local = datetime_hour_start + offset
    ) |>
    select(
      pid,
      platform,
      title_id,
      hour_start_local,
      hour_start_utc,
      minutes
    ) |>
    group_by(pid, platform, title_id, hour_start_local, hour_start_utc) |>
    summarise(minutes = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  hourly_telemetry <- bind_rows(hourly_from_sessions, hourly_from_steam)

  # Restrict each participant's data to the 91 days after their intake survey date.
  # Steam data is only available from intake onward, so using a symmetric window
  # would create unequal platform coverage across the pre/post period.
  intake_window <- intake |>
    select(pid, intake_date = date) |>
    mutate(pid = as.character(pid))

  hourly_telemetry <- hourly_telemetry |>
    left_join(intake_window, by = "pid") |>
    filter(
      as.numeric(difftime(
        as.Date(hour_start_local),
        intake_date,
        units = "days"
      )) >= 0,
      as.numeric(difftime(
        as.Date(hour_start_local),
        intake_date,
        units = "days"
      )) <= 91
    ) |>
    select(-intake_date)

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # DAILY (aggregated from hourly)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  daily_telemetry <- hourly_telemetry |>
    mutate(day_local = as.Date(hour_start_local)) |>
    group_by(pid, platform, day_local) |>
    summarise(minutes = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # WEEKLY (aggregated from daily)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  weekly_telemetry <- daily_telemetry |>
    mutate(week = floor_date(day_local, "week")) |>
    group_by(pid, platform, week) |>
    summarise(minutes = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # TELEMETRY SPANS (date ranges per participant/platform)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  telemetry_spans <- daily_telemetry |>
    group_by(pid, platform) |>
    summarise(
      telemetry_start = min(day_local, na.rm = TRUE),
      telemetry_end = max(day_local, na.rm = TRUE) + hours(1),
      week = floor_date(telemetry_end, "week"),
      n_weeks = as.integer(difftime(
        telemetry_end,
        telemetry_start,
        units = "weeks"
      )) +
        1,
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Save to cache
  dir.create(cache_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  saveRDS(
    list(
      sessions_telemetry = sessions_telemetry,
      hourly_telemetry = hourly_telemetry,
      daily_telemetry = daily_telemetry,
      weekly_telemetry = weekly_telemetry,
      telemetry_spans = telemetry_spans
    ),
    cache_file
  )
  message("Telemetry cache saved to ", cache_file)
}
```
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (Prepare NEET variables for analysis)"}
dat <- intake |>
  mutate(
    gender = case_when(
      gender %in% c("Man", "Male") ~ "Man",
      gender %in% c("Woman", "Female") ~ "Woman",
      gender == "Prefer not to say" ~ NA_character_,
      is.na(gender) ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ "Non-binary"
    ),
    # Ethnicity: harmonize UK/US categories
    ethnicity = case_when(
      ethnicity %in% c("White", "White alone") ~ "White",
      ethnicity %in%
        c(
          "Black",
          "Black or African American alone",
          "Black, African, Caribbean or Black British"
        ) ~ "Black",
      ethnicity %in%
        c("Asian", "Asian alone", "Asian or Asian British") ~ "Asian",
      ethnicity %in%
        c(
          "Mixed",
          "Mixed or multiple ethnic groups",
          "Two or More Races"
        ) ~ "Multiple",
      ethnicity %in% c("Prefer not to say") ~ NA_character_,
      is.na(ethnicity) ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    unspecified_unemployed = tolower(employment) %in%
      c(
        "unemployed but looking",
        "unemployed looking for work",
        "not currently employed",
        "working occasionally for a fast-food delivery service, but otherwise unemployed",
        "was working till start of april",
        "unemployment",
        "unable to work.",
        "unable to work",
        "no",
        "looking for work",
        "looking for employment",
        "looking for a job.",
        "looking for a job.",
        "still looking for part-time job",
        "seeking employment, but not currently employed",
        "don't work",
        "actively looking for work"
      ),

    full_time_caretaker = tolower(employment) %in%
      c(
        "unpaid carer",
        "i am not able tow ork as ihave autistic children i may ahve to go to school for any time so i am their fulltime care giver",
        "full-time unpaid carer",
        "full time unpaid carer",
        "full time carer for other household member",
        "full time carer for disabled partner",
        "full time carer",
        "family carer",
        "family caregiver",
        "carer for parent",
        "carer",
        "carer for my autistic child and have disability myself",
        "caregiver",
        "a homemaker or stay-at-home parent"
      ),

    disabled = tolower(employment) %in%
      c(
        "ssdi",
        "ssi",
        "registered disabled",
        "workers comp",
        "unemployed/sick/disabled",
        "unemployed disabled",
        "temporarily off work due to disability",
        "unable to work due to disability",
        "unable to work disabled",
        "unable to work due to ongoing condition",
        "i am unable to work due to ill health",
        "i am unable to work due to health issues",
        "long term sickness",
        "longterm sickness",
        "long term sick leave",
        "long term sick",
        "long term illness",
        "legally disabled/unable to work",
        "not working due to health reasons",
        "not currently working due to health issues",
        "non working due to disability",
        "disabled/unable to work.",
        "disabled/unable to work",
        "disabled/sick",
        "disabled/ unable to work",
        "disabled unemployed",
        "disabled unable to work",
        "disabled unable to work.",
        "disabled on benefits",
        "disabled and unable to work",
        "disabled and unemployed",
        "disabled and not currently employed",
        "disabled and chronic illness",
        "disabled",
        "disability",
        "disability benefits",
        "disable",
        "disabed",
        "chronically ill"
      ),
  ) |>
  mutate(
    neet = (unspecified_unemployed | full_time_caretaker | disabled) & age <= 25
  )
```
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (1:1 propensity score matching of NEET to non-NEET participants)"}
# Match each NEET participant to one non-NEET participant with similar age,
# gender, ethnicity, and country using nearest-neighbor PSM (logistic propensity model).
match_input <- dat |>
  filter(
    qualified,
    !is.na(age),
    !is.na(gender),
    !is.na(ethnicity),
    !is.na(country)
  )

m_out <- matchit(
  neet ~ age + gender + ethnicity + country,
  data = match_input,
  method = "nearest",
  distance = "glm",
  ratio = 1,
  replace = FALSE
)

summary(m_out, un = FALSE)

matched_pids <- match.data(m_out)$pid
dat_matched <- dat |> filter(pid %in% matched_pids)
```
:::



# Introduction

We compare gaming behaviour between young people who are NEET (not in education, employment, or training; age ≤ 25) and a 1:1 propensity-score-matched sample of non-NEET players matched on age, gender, ethnicity, and country, using platform telemetry (Steam, Xbox, Nintendo) restricted to the three months following each participant's intake survey (Steam data is only available from intake onward, so a symmetric pre/post window would create unequal platform coverage).

## Top games

The table below ranks the ten most-played games (by total playtime) separately for matched NEET and non-NEET players, alongside the number and percentage of each group who played the game, minutes per player, and genre metadata from IGDB.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
neet_totals <- dat_matched |>
  group_by(neet) |>
  summarise(total_players = n_distinct(pid))

# Derive genre, subgenre, and multiplayer labels from game metadata.
# games$original_name covers all platforms: game names for Steam/Nintendo, UUIDs for Xbox.
# IGDB data takes priority; Xbox-specific columns fill in where IGDB is missing.
game_meta <- games |>
  distinct(original_name, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  transmute(
    title_id = original_name,
    genre = coalesce(str_extract(genres, "^[^,]+"), xbox_primary_genre),
    subgenre = coalesce(
      str_extract(str_remove(genres, "^[^,]+,\\s*"), "^[^,]+"),
      xbox_subgenre
    ),
    multiplayer = game_modes
  )

# Use distinct(pid, neet) so each participant contributes one row to the join,
# avoiding inflation of play time by the number of biweekly survey responses.
top_games <- dat_matched |>
  distinct(pid, neet) |>
  left_join(hourly_telemetry, by = "pid") |>
  group_by(neet, title_id) |>
  summarise(
    total_minutes = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_players = n_distinct(pid),
    min_per_player = total_minutes / n_players,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  left_join(neet_totals, by = "neet") |>
  mutate(pct_players = 100 * (n_players / total_players)) |>
  left_join(game_meta, by = "title_id")

top_10 <- function(data, neet_val) {
  data |>
    filter(neet == neet_val, !is.na(title_id)) |>
    slice_max(n_players, n = 10) |>
    arrange(desc(n_players)) |>
    mutate(rank = row_number())
}

bind_rows(top_10(top_games, TRUE), top_10(top_games, FALSE)) |>
  select(
    rank,
    title_id,
    n_players,
    pct_players,
    total_minutes,
    min_per_player,
    genre,
    subgenre,
    multiplayer
  ) |>
  setNames(c(
    "Rank",
    "Game",
    "Players",
    "% Players",
    "Minutes",
    "Min/Player",
    "Genre",
    "Subgenre",
    "Mode(s)"
  )) |>
  tt(
    digits = 1,
    caption = "Top 10 games by total play time — matched NEET and non-NEET players"
  ) |>
  group_tt(i = list("NEET" = 1, "Non-NEET" = 11)) |>
  style_tt(fontsize = .8)
```

        const targetCell = table.querySelector(`[data-row="${i}"][data-col="${j}"]`);
        if (!targetCell) {
          console.warn(`Cell at (${i}, ${j}) not found.`);
        }

        // Get all cells that need to be removed
        const cellsToRemove = [];
        for (let r = 0; r < rowspan; r++) {
          for (let c = 0; c < colspan; c++) {
            if (r === 0 && c === 0) continue; // Skip the target cell
            const cell = table.querySelector(`[data-row="${i + r}"][data-col="${j + c}"]`);
            if (cell) {
              cellsToRemove.push(cell);

## Time of day

The figure below shows NEET players' playtime share relative to non-NEET players at each hour of the day. Positive values indicate hours where NEET players account for a larger share of play; negative values indicate hours where non-NEET players are relatively more active.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (time-of-day figure)"}
hourly_diff <- hourly_telemetry |>
  inner_join(dat_matched |> distinct(pid, neet), by = "pid") |>
  mutate(hour = hour(hour_start_local)) |>
  group_by(neet, hour) |>
  summarise(total_minutes = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(neet) |>
  mutate(prop = total_minutes / sum(total_minutes)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(neet, hour, prop) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = neet, values_from = prop, names_prefix = "neet_") |>
  # Difference in percentage points: NEET share minus non-NEET share
  mutate(diff_pp = 100 * (neet_TRUE - neet_FALSE))

ggplot(hourly_diff, aes(x = hour, y = diff_pp, fill = diff_pp > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.85) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4, colour = "grey30") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 21, by = 3),
    labels = c("12am", "3am", "6am", "9am", "12pm", "3pm", "6pm", "9pm")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = \(x) glue("{x} pp")) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "#E41A1C", "FALSE" = "#377EB8"),
    labels = c("TRUE" = "NEET higher", "FALSE" = "Non-NEET higher"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  labs(x = "Hour of day (local time)", y = "NEET \u2212 non-NEET (pp)") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![NEET minus non-NEET difference in share of total playtime per hour (percentage points, local time). Bars above zero indicate hours where NEET players are proportionally more active; bars below zero indicate the reverse.](index_files/figure-html/fig-time-of-day-1.png){#fig-time-of-day fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::


## Distributions

The raincloud plots below compare the matched groups on seven variables: objective playtime and session length from telemetry, self-reported play, gaming disorder symptoms (GDT, wave 1), life satisfaction, and the perceived value of gaming for work and daily routines.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (distribution raincloud plots)"}
# Telemetry hours per week: total minutes in the 91-day post-intake window
# divided by 13 (91 / 7) to give a comparable weekly rate across participants.
telemetry_hrs <- hourly_telemetry |>
  inner_join(dat_matched |> distinct(pid, neet), by = "pid") |>
  group_by(pid, neet) |>
  summarise(value = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE) / 60 / 13, .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(variable = "Telemetry playtime (hrs/wk)")

# Helper: biweekly data for matched participants with neet status attached.
# Using biweekly directly (rather than dat_matched) avoids column-name conflicts
# from the biweekly/intake join, which suffixes shared columns like life_sat to .x/.y.
bw_matched <- biweekly |>
  filter(pid %in% matched_pids) |>
  left_join(dat_matched |> distinct(pid, neet), by = "pid")

# Self-reported biweekly play: mean across waves, convert minutes → hrs/week
self_rep <- bw_matched |>
  group_by(pid, neet) |>
  summarise(
    value = mean(self_reported_biweekly_play, na.rm = TRUE) / 60 / 2,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(variable = "Self-reported play (hrs/wk)")

# Mean session length per person (Xbox and Nintendo only; Steam lacks session data).
# Apply the same post-intake 91-day restriction used for hourly_telemetry.
session_intake <- intake |>
  select(pid, intake_date = date) |>
  mutate(pid = as.character(pid))

session_len <- sessions_telemetry |>
  filter(platform %in% c("Xbox", "Nintendo")) |>
  inner_join(dat_matched |> distinct(pid, neet), by = "pid") |>
  left_join(session_intake, by = "pid") |>
  filter(
    as.numeric(difftime(as.Date(start_local), intake_date, units = "days")) >= 0,
    as.numeric(difftime(as.Date(start_local), intake_date, units = "days")) <= 91
  ) |>
  group_by(pid, neet) |>
  summarise(value = mean(duration_min, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(variable = "Session length (Xbox/Nintendo, min)")

# GDT mean at wave 1: recode text responses to numeric 1–5 then average items
gdt_levels <- c(
  "Never" = 1,
  "Rarely" = 2,
  "Sometimes" = 3,
  "Often" = 4,
  "Very often" = 5
)

gdt <- bw_matched |>
  filter(derived_wave == 1) |>
  mutate(across(gdt_1:gdt_4, ~ gdt_levels[.])) |>
  mutate(value = rowMeans(pick(gdt_1:gdt_4), na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  select(pid, neet, value) |>
  mutate(variable = "GDT mean (wave 1, 1\u20135)")

# Life satisfaction: mean across all available waves
life_sat_dat <- bw_matched |>
  group_by(pid, neet) |>
  summarise(value = mean(life_sat, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(variable = "Life satisfaction (0\u201310)")

# Gaming value (work): mean across all waves (-3 to 3)
gv_work <- bw_matched |>
  group_by(pid, neet) |>
  summarise(value = mean(gaming_value_work, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(variable = "Gaming value: work (\u22123\u20133)")

# Gaming value (routines): mean across all waves (-3 to 3)
gv_routines <- bw_matched |>
  group_by(pid, neet) |>
  summarise(
    value = mean(gaming_value_routines, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(variable = "Gaming value: routines (\u22123\u20133)")

plot_data <- bind_rows(
  telemetry_hrs,
  self_rep,
  session_len,
  gdt,
  life_sat_dat,
  gv_work,
  gv_routines
) |>
  filter(!is.na(value)) |>
  mutate(
    group = if_else(neet, "NEET", "Non-NEET"),
    variable = factor(
      variable,
      levels = c(
        "Telemetry playtime (hrs/wk)",
        "Self-reported play (hrs/wk)",
        "Session length (Xbox/Nintendo, min)",
        "GDT mean (wave 1, 1\u20135)",
        "Life satisfaction (0\u201310)",
        "Gaming value: work (\u22123\u20133)",
        "Gaming value: routines (\u22123\u20133)"
      )
    )
  )

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = value, y = group, colour = group, fill = group)) +
  # Mirrored dots: NEET faces downward, Non-NEET upward (no built-in summary point)
  stat_dots(
    aes(side = if_else(neet, "bottom", "top")),
    overflow = "compress",
    dotsize = 0.4,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  # Line connecting group means within each panel
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  # Mean points on the connecting line
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    colour = "black",
    size = 2.5,
    shape = 21,
    fill = "white",
    stroke = 1
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free_x", nrow = 3) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NEET" = "#E41A1C", "Non-NEET" = "#377EB8")) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("NEET" = "#E41A1C", "Non-NEET" = "#377EB8")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  guides(fill = "none", colour = "none") +
  theme(panel.spacing = unit(12, "pt"))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Raincloud plots comparing matched NEET and non-NEET players on objective playtime and session length (telemetry), self-reported play, gaming disorder symptoms (GDT, wave 1), life satisfaction, and perceived gaming value. Session length is based on Xbox and Nintendo data only (Steam provides hourly aggregates, not individual sessions). Dots show individual participants; lines connect group means.](index_files/figure-html/fig-distributions-1.png){#fig-distributions fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::


## Social play modes

The figure below compares the share of playtime spent in competitive multiplayer and cooperative games between matched NEET and non-NEET players. Games are tagged as competitive if IGDB lists Multiplayer, Massively Multiplayer Online, or Battle Royale modes, and as cooperative if Co-operative or Split screen modes are listed.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (social mode proportions)"}
# Tag each game title with competitive/cooperative flags
games_modes <- games |>
  mutate(
    is_competitive = !is.na(game_modes) &
      str_detect(
        game_modes,
        "Multiplayer|Massively Multiplayer Online|Battle Royale"
      ),
    is_cooperative = !is.na(game_modes) &
      str_detect(game_modes, "Co-operative|Split screen")
  ) |>
  summarise(
    is_competitive = any(is_competitive),
    is_cooperative = any(is_cooperative),
    .by = original_name
  )

# Per-person share of playtime in each mode, restricted to matched sample
mode_playtime_neet <- hourly_telemetry |>
  inner_join(dat_matched |> distinct(pid, neet), by = "pid") |>
  left_join(games_modes, by = c("title_id" = "original_name")) |>
  group_by(pid, neet) |>
  summarise(
    total_min = sum(minutes, na.rm = TRUE),
    competitive_min = sum(minutes[is_competitive == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE),
    cooperative_min = sum(minutes[is_cooperative == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    pct_competitive = 100 * competitive_min / total_min,
    pct_cooperative = 100 * cooperative_min / total_min
  )

# Bootstrap 95% CIs for mean % by NEET status and mode
set.seed(42)
mode_summary_neet <- mode_playtime_neet |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(pct_competitive, pct_cooperative),
    names_to = "mode",
    names_prefix = "pct_",
    values_to = "pct"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    mode = str_to_title(mode),
    group = if_else(neet, "NEET", "Non-NEET")
  ) |>
  group_by(mode, group) |>
  summarise(
    mean_pct = mean(pct, na.rm = TRUE),
    ci_lo = quantile(replicate(1000, mean(sample(pct, replace = TRUE))), 0.025),
    ci_hi = quantile(replicate(1000, mean(sample(pct, replace = TRUE))), 0.975),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-summary="Show code (social modes dotplot)"}
ggplot(mode_summary_neet, aes(x = mean_pct, y = group, colour = group)) +
  # Segment connecting NEET and Non-NEET means within each panel
  geom_line(aes(group = 1), colour = "grey70", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi), height = 0, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5) +
  facet_wrap(~mode, ncol = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("NEET" = "#E41A1C", "Non-NEET" = "#377EB8"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = \(x) glue("{x}%")) +
  labs(x = "Share of playtime", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey20", colour = "grey20"),
    strip.text = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold", size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11)
  )
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Share of playtime in competitive and cooperative game modes for matched NEET and non-NEET players. Panels are independent (a game can be both competitive and cooperative, so shares need not sum to 100%). Points show group means; horizontal lines show bootstrap 95% confidence intervals.](index_files/figure-html/fig-social-modes-1.png){#fig-social-modes fig-align='center' width=80%}
:::
:::
